desc: Makunduchi is a laid-back area on Zanzibar's southeast coast, known for broad tidal beaches, seaweed farms, and dhow-dotted horizons. It is best for slow island rhythms, kitesurfing seasons, and low-key beach stays rather than full-service resort nightlife.
hls: Long white-sand beach with dramatic tides
Authentic village atmosphere vs. larger resorts
Seaweed farming and local coastal livelihoods
Access to reef snorkeling and dhow trips

todo.name: Walk the southeast coast beach at low tide
todo.desc: See turquoise shallows and local seaweed farms exposed by the wide tidal range.

todo.name: Kitesurf lesson on the southeast coast
todo.desc: Book beginner or intermediate sessions when seasonal winds are most reliable.

todo.name: Snorkeling trip to nearby reefs
todo.desc: Join local boats to coral patches with tropical fish and clear-water conditions.

todo.name: Village cooking or culture tour
todo.desc: Learn Swahili ingredients, spice use, and daily life through community-led visits.

todo.name: Sunset dhow cruise
todo.desc: Sail traditional wooden boats along the coast for golden-hour views and relaxed pacing.

todo.name: Day trip to Paje or The Rock area
todo.desc: Combine neighboring beaches, cafés, and iconic offshore dining spots in one outing.

wifi.note: Beach lodges typically provide basic Wi-Fi; mobile data is often the more dependable option for calls and uploads.

prac.fltNote: Reach Makunduchi via Zanzibar Airport (ZNZ), then transfer about 1-1.5 hours by road. UK routings are usually one-stop via major Gulf, East African, or European hubs. Return fares are often ~£420-£700 off-peak, ~£700-£980 in shoulder demand, and ~£980+ around festive peaks.
prac.visa: Most visitors need a Tanzania eVisa or visa on arrival; confirm current entry and insurance requirements before travel.
alerts: Tidal swings are significant on the southeast coast; swimming windows shift by day.
Monsoon periods can bring heavy rain, rough seas, and slower boat-based activities.
ATM access is limited in village areas — carry enough cash for smaller local services.

sweet: June-October is usually drier and breezier for kitesurfing, while December-February can also be good for warm beach conditions between rains.
